titleStmt.title: SII 38.423: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 423. (A.R. No. 423 of 1912.) VAḺUVŪR, MAYAVARAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. On the south wall, maṇḍapa, infront of the central shrine, Vīraṭṭānēśvara temple.

summary.p: This inscription begins with the meykīrtti of the Chōḷa king Rājādhirāja II, Pūmannu padumam, the immediate predecessor of Kulōttuṅga III. The latter king adopted the first few times lines of the meykīrtti of Rājādhirāja in his inscriptions that were issued in the beginning years of his reign.

Students of Chōḷa history are aware of the fact that Rājarāja II (1146-1173 A.D.), the father of Kulōttuṅga III, had chosen his nephew Neriyuḍaiyapperumāḷ to succeed him as his two sons were too young to occupy the Chōḷa throne. Accordingly Neriyuḍaipperumāḷ ascended the throne with his coronation name Rājādhirāja in 1166 A.D. and ruled for sixteen years i.e. 1182 A.D. Rājādhirāja II passed on the throne to his cousin, Kulōttuṅga in 1178 A.D. when the latter came of the age. Both Rājādhirāja II and Kulōttuṅga III jointly ruled the Chōḷa kingdom for four years (1178-1182 A.D.). Hence, the inscriptions that were issued in the early years of Kulōttuṅga III contain a portion of the meykīrtti of Rājādhirāja II in the first few lines followed by his own meykīrtti. According to N. Sethuraman, this is an expression of the gratitude of king Kulōttuṅga III for Rājādhirāja II's loyalty to the Chōḷa kingdom, See N. Sethuraman, Saṅkarachōḻaṉ ulā Kulōttuṅga Chōḻaṉ-ulā pāṭṭuḍaittalaivargaḷ (1989).

This inscription is damaged and incomplete and it records that a lady ••••• ḍārch chāṉi, wife of Āṇḍān Bhaṭṭan son of Uḍumbai Maṇavāḷapperumāḷ of Nagarīśvarach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, consecrated an image of the god, Tiruchchattīmuṟṟamuḍaiyār in the 29th regnal year of the king (1207 A.D.) in the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭāṉam-uḍaiyār at Vaḻugūr, a brahmadēya village in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. She is also stated to have gifted an amount of 13,200 kāśu to the temple to meet the expenses relating to the consecration, and the expenses for daily offerings and worship and also for the performance of other rituals. This amount was deposited in the temple treasury and the interest was meant to provide for these expenses. But for some reasons the charity was hindered from continuing, commencing from Aiṟpasi-vishu, and she appears them to have made gift of a further fourteen-thousand kāśu. The temple officials śrīmāhēśvarakkaṇkāṇi, śrīkāriyam cheyvar, and dēvakanmi appear to have undertaken to carry out the charity.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . pūmaruviya ticaimukantoṉRead ticaimukattoṉ paṭaitta peru
vicaṅkaDo viḷaṅka temaruviya pacuntuḷavat tiruneṭumālivaneṉṉa nīrā
cūḻnta vaḷaiyāḻi neṭumpuṟattu Orāḻi taṉinaṭātta Utaiya kulattavatarittu
maṇṭalameṉa viḷaṅkat taṉikkuṭaikkiḻiṉitiruntu Iṉtirapatam Aḷittaruḷum I
ṉa Ivaneṉṉavum Amaittuc cakkaramuñceṅkolun tikkaṉaittuñ cela naṭāttakka
paṭi kavippa poṟuppamainta muṭipuṉaintu mīṉavaṉ perāl tecamellām vāḻvali ma
ruḷi taṉi Āṇmai viṭṭāṇmai cetu vaṭamana naraittiṟaippa mātta mu
ci pukkumumiḻa tiraicaiyun tiṟai kavaṟntu tāṅ rum por vaṭukai ven
kai maṇṭalantaṉatākki ponmaṭai petuṟantai yeṉṉun nakaram puka
ṇṭoṉṉāl vaḻuti maintaṉai mūkkarintu tamuḻRead tamiḻmaturaikoṇṭu ceyya tiruvaṭiyi
tāyaroṭum Āparaṇaṅkuṭuttaruḷi tiruvaṭiyil teva(va) nāyakaneṉṉum pe
ṭaya Ulakamuḻutuṭaiyār tiruvāṇaiuṭaiyār ceyam toḷvali ceyalāka cem
virasiṁhāsanattuppuvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecaripaṉ
maraāṉa tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyumīḻamuṅ karuvūrum pāṇṭiyaṉai muṭittalaiyu
ṅ koṇṭaruḷi virāviṣekamum vijaiyābhiṣekamum paṇṇi Aruḷiṉa tiripuvaṉaviratevaṟ
kki yāṇṭu muppattumunṟāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷaṉāṭṭut tiruvaḻuntūrnāṭ[ ṭu brahma]
deyam vaḻukūr Uṭaiyār tiruvīraṭṭāṉamuṭaiyār koyil melait tirunaṭaimāḷikai
yil viṉāyakappiḷḷaiyārkku vaṭakkil(p) pattiyil nakariīśvarac caruppetimaṅkalat
muḻutuṭaicceri Uṭumpai maṇavāḷapperumāḷ makan Āṇṭān paṭṭan brāhmaṇi ma
ṭārccāṉi Eḻuṉtaruḷivitta Uṭaiyār tiruccattimuṟṟa muṭaiyārkkut tiruppidraṣṭai A
mutu paṭikkum Uṭalākat tiruppidraṣṭai paṇṇiṉa Irupattonpatāvatu cittirai ma
nāḷ munnūṟṟu Iraṇṭuvaraikkuḷḷu patinmuvvāyirattu Irunūṟu kācum pati
llum cīpaṇṭāratte pukuntu Ikkācilum nellilum ṉāyaṉārai Immaṇṭai Ā
ṉitā Eḻuntaruḷivittu varukaiyā ṭṭattukkum Eḻuntaruḷi Iraṇ
veṇṭum kallum tiruppidraṣṭaikku veṇṭum Aḻivukkum muppattiraṇṭāvatu na Āṟu
māvu varai tiruppaṭimāṟṟukkum viṉañaṉattukkum veṇṭum kācu nikki ninṟa ka
policaikku kuṭuttu nāṭṭu koṇṭu kiṭantamaiyil Aṟpaciviṣu mutal Uṭai
ppūcaittaṭṭu paṭumaḷavāy Irukkaiyiṉāl mākeśvavarar piḷḷai
nāṭṭukkoṇṭu kiṭanta kācu pūcaikku Uṭalāka pukuntu ci paṇ
ṉam paṇṭāra Upaiyamāka kalveṭṭu peṟaveṇumeṉṟu Aṟivittu
maiyil Ippirāmaṇi kācu paṇṭārattu muppattu munṟāvatu paṅku
pat tiṟappai kuṭutta kācil piṉ Āṟumātattu viñcaṉaṅkaḷukku U
Aiyyāyiramum Āka kācu patinālāyiramum paṇṇi Upaiyamā
�kka nāḷ Oṉṟukku tiruvamutaruci naāṉāḻiyāka vakuttu pakaṟ A
pūccum viṣuvayaṉa kirāṇaṅkaḷ Uḷḷiṭṭa tivasaṅkaḷukku
ṭaiyārkku ttāmam tiruma(ai)ñcaṉam Uḷḷiṭṭaṉavaiyuñ cantirātittavarai paṇṭārat�
vatāka kalveṭṭuvikkakaṭavomānom cimākeśvarak kaṅkāṇi ceyvānum cikāriyañ cevvānum tevakanmi
Digital edition of SII 38.423 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
